holdings.note: SpaceX — IPO'd Jun 2026; FMP may not yet cover earnings/dividend dates for a brand-new listing.

macro.label: Triple witching
macro.detail: Quarterly simultaneous expiry of index futures, index options & single-stock options — volume/volatility spike and pinning risk in the most options-heavy names.

INTC.read: INTC is +7.5% on the day and +7% on the week with no dated company catalyst on the board — that's a momentum move you're not getting paid to hold through if there's no earnings or product event to anchor it. With the next concrete event being triple witching ~80 days out, the window favors taking some off into strength rather than pressing an 8-share position that's already extended. Wait for either a confirmed earnings date or a pullback to re-engage with conviction.
INTC.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (macro only; no dated INTC company catalyst)
INTC.bull: Foundry/AI narrative re-rate continues and the squeeze extends until an earnings date prints to validate it.
INTC.bear: No company catalyst means this is pure flow/sentiment; a gap-fill back through the breakout level erases the week quickly.

NVDA.read: No dated company catalyst on the board for NVDA right now — the next earnings print isn't loaded and the only thing ahead is triple witching ~80 days out, which is a tape event, not a stock-specific one. Tape action is constructive but unremarkable (+1.8% on the day, roughly flat on the week near $198), so there's no setup pulling you to act on either side. This is a wait-for-the-next-confirmed-catalyst window, not an add or trim window.
NVDA.bull: AI capex cycle intact and price action is holding the highs without any dated event risk to fade into.
NVDA.bear: No company catalyst loaded means any move is positioning-driven; an options-expiry tape event could whip an 8-share, stretched-multiple name.

ORCL.read: ORCL is down 11.2% over the past week with no company-specific dated catalyst on the board to anchor a window — the only thing on the calendar is triple witching ~80 days out, which is noise, not a thesis. Without a confirmed earnings date or other binary event, the sharp drawdown is hard to fade or chase responsibly; the right move is to wait for the next print to be dated before sizing a view. A stabilization in price action or a confirmed earnings date would be the trigger to convert this to add-on-weakness.
ORCL.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (macro only; no ORCL-specific catalyst on calendar)
ORCL.bull: OCI/AI capacity demand and RPO backlog remain the structural story; a 11% pullback into the next earnings print could reset expectations favorably.
ORCL.bear: No near-term dated catalyst to break the downtrend, and a -11% week often signals fundamental concerns (capex, margin, hyperscaler competition) the next print has to answer.

NOK.read: No dated company catalyst on the board for NOK — no confirmed earnings or ex-div in the feed — so there's no specific window to lean into here. Price is drifting (-3.7% on the week, +1.4% today) on a small 174-share position, which doesn't force a decision. The only thing ahead is triple witching ~80 days out, which is a market-structure event, not a NOK-specific driver.
NOK.bull: Quality telecom-infra name basing in the low-$13s with no near-term binary risk to fade into.
NOK.bear: Without a dated catalyst, the position drifts with tape and FX with no clear re-rating trigger.

NBIS.read: NBIS is ripping (+7.3% today on a quiet tape with no dated company catalyst on the board), which is the hard setup: momentum is doing the work but there's no print or event to anchor an add/trim window to. With no confirmed earnings date and only a distant triple-witching as the macro marker, the honest move is to let the tape define the next level rather than chase strength or fade it blind. A confirmed next earnings date would convert this from a momentum watch into an actionable window.
NBIS.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (macro only; no company catalyst on calendar)
NBIS.bull: AI-infra momentum name extending on strong tape with no near-term binary risk to derail it.
NBIS.bear: Single-day +7% on no dated catalyst leaves the position exposed to a momentum unwind with no event to underwrite the drawdown.

KO.read: Tiny 5-share KO position with no company-specific dated catalyst on the board — no confirmed earnings or ex-div in the feed, and the only macro marker is triple witching ~80 days out, which is noise for a defensive staple like KO. Price action is unremarkable (-1% on the day, +1.8% on the week), so there's no stretched-into-print or capitulation setup to lean on. Without a dated event, this is a sit-and-wait; revisit when the next earnings date or ex-div gets confirmed.
KO.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (macro only; no KO-specific catalyst on the calendar)
KO.bull: Defensive cash-flow compounder with steady dividend; quiet tape with no binary risk in view.
KO.bear: No near-term catalyst to re-rate the name; opportunity cost while staples trade sideways.

SPCX.read: Tiny 10-share position in a freshly public name (SpaceX IPO'd Jun 2026) with no confirmed earnings or dividend dates in the feed yet — aggregators typically lag new listings by a quarter or two. With nothing dated to trade against, the only near-term marker is Sept 18 triple witching, which can amplify volatility in low-float recent IPOs but isn't a fundamental catalyst. Sit on hands until the first earnings date gets confirmed or a lockup/secondary headline gives a real window.
SPCX.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (only dated event available; no confirmed earnings yet)
SPCX.bull: Scarcity bid on the only liquid SpaceX proxy as index inclusion and first earnings print come into view.
SPCX.bear: Post-IPO lockup expiries and option-expiry volatility can drive sharp drawdowns with no earnings anchor to defend the price.

disclaimer: Forward earnings dates are third-party aggregator estimates, not company-confirmed — confirm against the company's IR release (~2 weeks ahead). Dividends, macro prints and witching are scheduled/confirmed. Not investment advice.
